Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Understand User Input: Input Format: The user provides a prompt describing how a DECLARE model is represented in pm4py as a Python dictionary. Keys: Specific DECLARE constraint types: existence, absence, exactly_one, init, responded_existence, coexistence, response, precedence, succession, altresponse, altprecedence, altsuccession, chainresponse, chainprecedence, chainsuccession, noncoexistence, nonsuccession, nonchainsuccession. Values: For unary constraints, dict mapping activities to support, confidence. For binary constraints, dict mapping activity pairs to support, confidence. Scenario: A product design and launch process with 10 activities: IG, DD, TFC, CE, PC, LT, UT, AG, MP, FL. Task: Construct a Python dictionary representing the DECLARE model for this scenario.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Scenario & Derive Constraints: I need to logically infer reasonable DECLARE constraints based on the described process flow. Activities: IG, DD, TFC, CE, PC, LT, UT, AG, MP, FL. Logical flow: IG happens first (init), DD follows IG, TFC and CE likely happen after DD, PC happens after TFC and CE, LT and UT happen after PC, AG happens after LT and UT, MP can happen after AG, FL happens after MP and AG. Lets map this to DECLARE constraints: init: IG, existence: all activities, exactly_one: all activities, absence: empty, response: (IG, DD), (DD, TFC), (DD, CE), (TFC, PC), (CE, PC), (PC, LT), (PC, UT), (LT, AG), (UT, AG), (AG, MP), (MP, FL), precedence: reverse pairs, succession: (IG, DD), (MP, FL), coexistence: LT and UT, TFC and CE, nonsuccession: (FL, IG), responded_existence: some pairs.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: response: (IG, DD), (DD, TFC), (DD, CE), (TFC, PC), (CE, PC), (PC, LT), (PC, UT), (LT, AG), (UT, AG), (AG, MP), (MP, FL), precedence: (DD, IG), (TFC, DD), (CE, DD), (PC, TFC), (PC, CE), (LT, PC), (UT, PC), (AG, LT), (AG, UT), (MP, AG), (FL, MP), succession: (IG, DD) and (MP, FL), coexistence: (LT, UT), (TFC, CE), responded_existence: (IG, DD), (DD, TFC), etc, nonsuccession: (FL, IG), (FL, DD). All others empty. Values: [1.0, 1.0].

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Structure the Dictionary according to Prompt: The prompt specifies exact keys. I must include all of them, even if empty dicts. Lets draft it carefully: declare_model with existence populated for all acts, absence empty, exactly_one all acts, init IG, responded_existence some pairs, coexistence pairs, response pairs, precedence reverse pairs, succession some, altresponse empty, etc, noncoexistence empty, nonsuccession some pairs.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 4. Refine & Check against Prompt Requirements: Verify prompt format, use tuples for binary, all 18 keys present, unary activities to [1.0, 1.0], binary pairs to [1.0, 1.0], scenario flow IG -> DD -> (TFC, CE) -> PC -> (LT, UT) -> AG -> MP -> FL justifies constraints, coexistence for parallels, response precedence for flow.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 5. Draft Response (Mental): Present Python dictionary with all keys, comments for rationale, ensure format matches pm4py tuples and lists, confidence 1.0 for strict rules, structure output with code block and explanation. Self-Correction: Ensure all keys explicit, map business logic.
